# Curator

> Curator is a fast, native desktop app for macOS, Windows and Linux that renames messy media downloads into clean, correctly-named TV and film files and files them into a browsable collection. It is a modern, lightweight alternative to FileBot. Pricing is $9.99/year or $80 once, with a 14-day free trial. Curator is launching soon — you can register your interest at https://yourcurator.app/#download to be told when it ships.

Curator matches files against TheTVDB, TheMovieDB and Trakt, shows a confidence score for every match, and offers a poster picker when it is unsure. It tracks collection completion, reports the episodes you are missing, and includes a full-screen presentation mode. It is built in Rust (around 12 MB, no Java, no bundled web browser), and your library stays on your own machine.

## Pages

- [Home](https://yourcurator.app/): Overview, features, pricing and download.
- [Curator vs FileBot](https://yourcurator.app/vs-filebot/): Honest comparison with FileBot, including where each tool is stronger.
- [Contact](https://yourcurator.app/contact/): How to reach the team (support@yourcurator.app).
- [Terms & Conditions](https://yourcurator.app/terms/): Licence and terms of use.
- [Privacy Policy](https://yourcurator.app/privacy/): What data is collected and how it is handled.

## Key facts

- Status: launching soon — not yet downloadable; register interest at https://yourcurator.app/#download.
- Platforms: macOS, Windows and Linux.
- Pricing (from launch): $9.99/year or $80 one-time for the current major version; 14-day free trial, no card required.
- Metadata sources: TheTVDB, TheMovieDB and Trakt.
- Licence covers 2 computers and is offline-verifiable (no phone-home lockout).
- Category: media file renamer and collection manager; a FileBot alternative.
